title: abutilon inaequale
intro: Abutilon inaequale, also known as the Indian mallow or Abutilon, is a plant species in the Malvaceae family, native to tropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Abutilon inaequale include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and aiding in the treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and ulcers. Abutilon inaequale can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
